market_context: US markets are CLOSED today (July 4 falls on Saturday -> holiday observed Friday); orders can fill Monday Jul 6. Thursday validated the rotation call: Dow +539 to a record close led by Apple/McDonald's/Disney, S&P flat, Nasdaq -0.8% with chips down a second day (MU -7%, AMAT -7.4%, AMD -4.3%) — but QCOM rose against the tide. June jobs came in soft at 57k vs ~114k expected, pushing back Fed-hike talk. Next week: big-bank earnings kick off Jul 14 (JPM held), CPI lands the same week.

position_actions.action: CANCEL or HALVE the buy-limit 2.05 @ $185 (T212 ticker YNDX)
position_actions.reason: Downgrade from yesterday's 'keep, eyes open': Nebius fell 17%+ on the Meta cloud-competition report and bearish takes are multiplying ('the selloff exposes a deeper problem' — Seeking Alpha). The GPU-scarcity premium underpinning its valuation is exactly what Meta threatens. You already own this theme via CoreWeave with a planned add at 65. Adding a THIRD tranche of AI-infra exposure at $185 with no dated catalyst until August earnings is concentration, not conviction. Watchlist trigger: reclaim of $230.

new_plays.name: Rivian Automotive

tranches.condition: first pullback into 18.4-18.7 (do NOT chase above 19)
deeper fade to 17.9-18.1 if the gap partially fills

new_plays.catalyst: Q2 deliveries beat (12,194) + FY26 guidance RAISED to 65-70k vehicles; Q2 earnings Jul 30 is the timer
new_plays.risk_reward: from ~18.3 entry: risk -10.4% to stop, +17.5% to first sell (1:1.7), +28% to full exit (1:2.7)
new_plays.checklist_score: 4/6 (catalyst, sentiment split, timing, R:R pass; analyst-upside unverified; valuation ~3x sales is cheap vs Tesla but the company is still unprofitable)
new_plays.thesis: Real dated catalyst chain: Q2 deliveries beat expectations, full-year guidance raised on R1/EDV/R2 momentum, stock +13% Thursday on volume with shorts still engaged (fuel). $4.84B cash plus a $1B Volkswagen investment removes near-term survival risk. Framework rule applies: it jumped ~13-15% on the day, so wait for the first pullback — the play is the drift from the delivery beat into Jul 30 earnings. Also the first position in the book outside tech/financials.
new_plays.risks: Deliveries are not profits — the Jul 30 print shows margins and could unwind the pop; EV demand is macro-sensitive; short-seller theses are active. If it never pulls back into the zone, let it go — no chasing.
